title: 31St Century Fox

jokes.text: One hundred! That's almost ten per year.
jokes.setup: The Professor announces their 100th delivery as a milestone achievement
jokes.punchline: Bender's math reveals this averages to pathetically few deliveries per year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his enthusiasm paired with accidentally revealing their incompetence. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I'm ordering a hundred kegs, a hundred hookers and a hundred Elvis impersonators who aren't above a little hooking, should the occasion arise.
jokes.setup: Bender wants to celebrate with a party
jokes.punchline: The escalation from beer to hookers to Elvis impersonators who double as hookers
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender excess with a creative twist on Elvis impersonators. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: she prefers it in soufflé form
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor takes nitroglycerin for her heart condition
jokes.punchline: Being a 'class act,' she takes explosive medicine in fancy French pastry form
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - taking explosive heart medication but making it 'classy.' Great setup for the episode's central gag.

jokes.text: Bam..
jokes.setup: Elzar is explaining the dangerous soufflé
jokes.punchline: He delivers his signature catchphrase in the most inappropriate, dangerous context possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Elzar can't help but say his catchphrase even when discussing explosives. Great timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Wow, it's ricketier than it looks.
jokes.setup: They approach what's described as a 'rickety rope bridge'
jokes.punchline: Somehow it manages to be even more unstable than its already terrible appearance suggested
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag - competent but not particularly creative. Works as setup for the physical comedy to follow.

jokes.text: I shall inform Mrs. Astor that the circus is in town.
jokes.setup: Bender presumably arrives in some ridiculous state after his jump attempt
jokes.punchline: The butler's deadpan, proper announcement treating the disaster as merely entertainment
jokes.explanation: Great butler character work - maintaining propriety while delivering a subtle burn. Good economy and voice.

jokes.text: Where's the exploding? One does not explode in Mrs. Astor's face.
jokes.setup: The soufflé was supposed to contain explosive nitroglycerin
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Astor treats the lack of explosion as a breach of etiquette rather than relief
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Mrs. Astor's aristocratic propriety applied to explosions. The formal language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, I have. And I'm his uncle.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor notes the Farnsworths have been in New New York for almost 200 years
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth claims he personally has been there 200 years, then introduces himself as his own uncle
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth confusion about his own age and family relationships. The absurd logic is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I can eat a hot dog underwater.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor comments on his unconventional nature
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth offers this completely random and useless skill as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Farnsworth non-sequitur. The specificity and uselessness of the skill make it hilarious. Great economy.

jokes.text: We can take them.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor invites them to a fancy charity event with 'all the best families'
jokes.punchline: Bender interprets this as a challenge to fight rather than a social invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender misunderstanding - he always assumes conflict. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: the land liner struck a mailbox and went down
jokes.setup: Describing the land Titanic disaster in dramatic terms
jokes.punchline: This massive, supposedly unsinkable vessel was defeated by a simple mailbox
jokes.explanation: Great Titanic parody - the bathos of a grand tragedy caused by hitting a mailbox. Good setup for the episode's themes.

jokes.text: Mutants? That's the kind of thing you are.
jokes.setup: Learning about the mutant charity fund
jokes.punchline: Amy's casual, tactless observation about Leela's identity
jokes.explanation: Good Amy moment showing her tactless nature, but relatively simple joke structure.

jokes.text: Ooh! Shut up.
jokes.setup: The charity film describes mutants as 'horrific monsters'
jokes.punchline: Amy's inappropriate excited reaction followed by Leela's sharp correction
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment - Amy's obliviousness to the offense she's causing. Works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: At brown university, the nation's premiere institution of lower learning.
jokes.setup: Describing where mutants learn their skills
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'brown' (sewage/Ivy League) and 'lower' (geographical/academic quality)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on Brown University and sewer themes. Multiple layers of meaning work well together.

jokes.text: Not in person, thank God.
jokes.setup: Suggesting poor mutants will thank donors for their charity
jokes.punchline: The immediate clarification that personal contact with mutants would be awful
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical commentary on performative charity. The immediate undercut works perfectly.

jokes.text: Really? It's also about keeping those filthy things busy.
jokes.setup: Defending the university as more than just sewer maintenance
jokes.punchline: Revealing her second motivation is equally dehumanizing
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mrs. Astor's casual bigotry. The 'correction' that isn't actually better is effective.

jokes.text: My great uncle once saw a rat.
jokes.setup: Talking about mutants breeding like rats
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously minimal connection to the topic through distant family rodent encounter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant aristocratic disconnect - treating a distant rat sighting as relevant expertise. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I might say it with my evening boot.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor is getting angry about Leela's comments
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific threat involving formal footwear
jokes.explanation: Good aristocratic character quirk - the specificity of 'evening boot' as a weapon is amusing and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well! Rarely have I never!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor is expressing outrage
jokes.punchline: The mangled version of 'Never have I ever' that accidentally says the opposite
jokes.explanation: Decent malapropism that fits the character's flustered aristocratic nature. The double negative creates accidental meaning.

jokes.text: You may have eluded the authorities, but don't nothing get past Mrs. Astor.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor is confronting the escaped Leela
jokes.punchline: The grammatically incorrect double negative while boasting about her intelligence
jokes.explanation: Simple grammar joke that undermines her claim to superiority. Works but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: this hellhole feels more like a nice, regular hole.
jokes.setup: Leela calling the sewer a hellhole
jokes.punchline: Her father's touching but absurd attempt to reframe it positively
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with good wordplay. The father's optimism about their terrible situation is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: sewer coffee, sewer cake and safeway ice cream
jokes.setup: Suggesting they celebrate Leela's arrival
jokes.punchline: The progression from sewer-themed items to inexplicably normal grocery store ice cream
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor in the list. The Safeway ice cream is oddly specific but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: You did ruin her life. / I am right.
jokes.setup: Fry feeling guilty about ruining Leela's life
jokes.punchline: Hermes' blunt confirmation and self-satisfaction about being correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes moment - his bureaucratic bluntness and satisfaction with being technically correct. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Wait. You knowingly attempted to harbor a mutant?! / We did harbor a mutant!
jokes.setup: Hermes accidentally reveals they knew Leela was a mutant
jokes.punchline: The Mayor's outrage followed by Hermes doubling down on their guilt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Hermes makes things worse. His bureaucratic honesty working against them is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, they're with the chuds now.
jokes.setup: The surface dwellers have been sentenced to the sewers
jokes.punchline: Casual use of the derogatory term 'chuds' to describe the surface people
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal using a typically derogatory term. Shows the mutants' perspective on surface dwellers. Good economy.

jokes.text: You'll grow a camel hump or a Zoidberg face.
jokes.setup: Warning about DNA mutation from toxic waste
jokes.punchline: Treating Zoidberg's face as equivalent to a deformity
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke treating a main character's appearance as a horrible mutation. Shows the universe's attitude toward Zoidberg.

jokes.text: Um, I would, but I j... I ate a bunch of potato salad, so...
jokes.setup: Leela challenges Fry to jump in toxic waste to understand mutant life
jokes.punchline: Fry's pathetic, obviously false excuse about potato salad
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Fry's cowardice. The potato salad excuse is appropriately lame and character-fitting.

jokes.text: You are all surface.
jokes.setup: Leela is angry at Fry's refusal to understand mutant struggles
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'surface' as both literal location and shallow personality
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that works on multiple levels. Perfect economy and fits the show's surface/underground themes perfectly.

jokes.text: I trust the orgy pit has been scraped and buttered?
jokes.setup: Bender is preparing for his party
jokes.punchline: The casual discussion of orgy pit maintenance as if it's normal catering prep
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender excess treated matter-of-factly. The specificity of 'scraped and buttered' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: the biggest and onlyest land boat ever constructed
jokes.setup: Describing the land Titanic wreck
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's made-up word 'onlyest' and the contradiction it reveals
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg malapropism that accidentally reveals logical contradiction. The made-up superlative is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hand me some more of that cruise director.
jokes.setup: They're using remains from the shipwreck
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg casually requesting human remains as tools, identified by job title
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with Zoidberg's casual attitude toward death. The job title identification adds specificity.

jokes.text: It's jewelry... jewelry. It's jewelry, people!
jokes.setup: Amy discovers something valuable in the wreck
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly excited repetition of 'jewelry' showing her materialistic priorities
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Amy's materialism. The repetition works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Did you guys know I have a crush on Leela?
jokes.setup: Fry is expressing his feelings about the valuable ring
jokes.punchline: His completely oblivious question about something that's been obvious for years
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's obliviousness. The audience has known this for years, making his 'revelation' funny.

jokes.text: Have some more of what looks like lemonade.
jokes.setup: Mother offering refreshments in the sewer
jokes.punchline: The uncertainty about whether it's actually lemonade given their location
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment about sewer dining. The careful phrasing suggests it's probably not lemonade.

jokes.text: this is the dangest wang I ever doodled
jokes.setup: The Professor commenting on Bender's party
jokes.punchline: The old man using slang inappropriately while maintaining his proper speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Farnsworth trying to use youth slang. The formal delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I said scram, Grapey!
jokes.setup: Bender wants to be alone after his party
jokes.punchline: He's apparently talking to a grape, treating it like a person he wants to get rid of
jokes.explanation: Amusing visual gag showing Bender's post-party depression. The personification of fruit is absurd and character-fitting.

jokes.text: I tried to scream, but I barfed.
jokes.setup: Fry jumped into toxic waste and is waiting to see if he mutated
jokes.punchline: His physical reaction prevented the dramatic scream he was going for
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy undermining a dramatic moment. The honesty about his unglamorous reaction is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Uh... hug. Handshake. I'll text you.
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela have a romantic moment after his sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Fry's rapid backing down from physical intimacy through increasingly distant options
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Fry's romantic cowardice. The progression from kiss to text is perfectly structured.

jokes.text: We can do that, too...
jokes.setup: Fry talks about gazing at the sky in wonder without wet clumps falling in your mouth
jokes.punchline: The mutant's weak attempt to claim they can also do this despite living in sewers
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as the mutant tries to maintain dignity despite obvious impossibility. The trailing off works well.

jokes.text: Play 'Whip it!' No. Play the other one.
jokes.setup: Devo is performing for the mutants
jokes.punchline: The crowd wants their most famous song, but Devo rejects it for a deep cut
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about bands avoiding their hits. Works as commentary on artistic integrity vs. popular demand.

jokes.text: But who could possibly bend such a huge pipe?
jokes.setup: Need someone to bend massive pipe
jokes.punchline: Obvious setup for Bender, whose name and primary ability is bending
jokes.explanation: Very predictable setup for Bender joke. Does the job but lacks any cleverness or surprise.

jokes.text: What goes down must back up.
jokes.setup: Bender bends the pipe to return sewage to the surface
jokes.punchline: Sewage-specific twist on 'what goes up must come down'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun adapting a familiar phrase to the sewage context. Not brilliant but well-constructed and fits the moment.

jokes.text: I picked the wrong day to wear my new crocses.
jokes.setup: Sewage is backing up onto the streets
jokes.punchline: The combination of 'Crocs' footwear and the situation, creating 'crocses'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay combining Crocs shoes with the sewage situation. Simple but functional joke.

jokes.text: and the blood of your first-born children. That's so we have something to give up in the negotiations.
jokes.setup: Leela lists mutant demands including equal rights
jokes.punchline: The horrible demand followed by the practical explanation of negotiation strategy
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and commentary on negotiation tactics. The logical explanation for the absurd demand is brilliant.

jokes.text: Ah, so what? I have a chandelier in my car made of those.
jokes.setup: They present her with the precious gemerald ring
jokes.punchline: Her casual dismissal revealing even more excessive wealth
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing her ridiculous wealth. The car chandelier detail is appropriately excessive.

jokes.text: My mother's nuts.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor's daughter asks why she never heard this story
jokes.punchline: The casual dismissal of her mother's entire emotional revelation
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan understatement that deflates the emotional moment. Perfect economy and generational disconnect.

jokes.text: Are we not men now? I'm 40% potato, but close enough.
jokes.setup: Celebrating their freedom with Devo's famous question
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal interpretation and his percentage-based self-description
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's recurring gag while playing with the Devo reference. Character-consistent and well-timed.

jokes.text: Hooray! A happy ending for the rich people!
jokes.setup: Mr. and Mrs. Astor are reunited
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sarcastic comment about who actually benefits from this 'happy' ending
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta commentary on storytelling priorities. Zoidberg cuts through the sentiment to point out the class implications.

jokes.text: Was there a fire?
jokes.setup: Leela is showing her parents where she works
jokes.punchline: Her father's assumption that the Planet Express building's condition indicates disaster
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about the building's poor condition. The innocent question works as mild commentary on their workplace.

tmdb_overview: After saving a robotic fox from hunters, Bender becomes the hunted.
